title: nanousd Read-Only C API Backend Capability
purpose: Define the bounded read-only contract for generating a backend that serves nanousd's C API from the skillgraph USD layer, composition, stage, path, token, listOp, and typed-value contracts.

included: exported nanousd_create_backend_v1 entry point with C linkage
complete NanousdBackend_v1 function pointer population with no null slots
crash-safe C ABI behavior for null, invalid, malformed, missing, and unsupported inputs
generated portable CMake build metadata for the C++ backend and adapter
NanousdBackend_v1 vtable population for read-only functions
deterministic unsupported stubs for mutating functions
runtime stage-open and traversal behavior for supported local USDA fixtures through usd-composed-stage-open
composed stage-open result consumption before nanousd stage handle construction
stage lifecycle over a generated read-only stage object
stage validity, error, root-layer path, and contributing layer enumeration
stage metadata reads for numeric, string, and token-like authored fields
prim traversal by index, path, defaultPrim, children, and child name
prim path, name, typeName, kind, active, specifier-derived defined/abstract, and instanceable queries
attribute enumeration, type-name query, authored default reads, and connection target reads
typed scalar attribute reads for bool, integer, int64, float, double, string, token, and asset values
typed vector, quaternion, matrix3d, matrix4d, and homogeneous array reads from foundational typed payload metadata
time sample key enumeration and exact authored sample reads for supported scalar, vector, and homogeneous array values
relationship target enumeration
path handle parse/string/parent/name/append/equality predicates with real typed handles by delegating to usd-paths
standalone listOp create/introspection/combine/free utilities and source-visible authored listOp reads by delegating to usd-listops-authored
stateless vector, matrix, and quaternion math utilities where the C API operates only on caller-provided arrays
deferred: all C API setters, create functions, clear/block functions, reference/relationship writes, variant writes, schema registration, and file serialization
USDC and USDZ parsing or writing
full asset resolver behavior beyond the existing resource and layer-open contracts
full runtime composition of recursive or chained sublayers, references, payloads, and variant-contained arcs behind nanousd_open
recursive value resolution, interpolation, clips, spline evaluation, and schema fallback values
schema registry semantics for isa/hasapi beyond direct authored type/API metadata visibility
zero-copy arraydata pointers when the generated storage is not already contiguous for the requested element type
xformOp stack evaluation and composed local transform calculation

required.reason: The backend open path must consume a graph-owned composed stage-open result that discovers and opens direct local composition dependencies before stage population.
The C API stage and prim handles expose populated stage traversal and query indexes.
Attribute, relationship, and metadata reads consume typed source-visible field values.
Typed C API readers depend on scalar domain, shape, matrix, quaternion, semantic alias, array, dictionary, and valueBlock payload metadata.
NanousdPath operations and relationship target output delegate to graph path identity.
Token-valued attribute and metadata reads preserve token identity internally while exposing C strings at the C boundary.
Prim listOp read/introspection consumes the authored listOp model rather than parsing adapter dictionaries.

implemented_readonly: prim traversal and prim field queries
attribute enumeration, type query, scalar/vector/quaternion/matrix/array default reads
time sample key enumeration and exact sample reads
path utilities with total null-safe companion functions
listOp create/read/introspection/combine utilities with total null-safe companion functions

value_mapping.scalar_reads: Nanousd scalar readers consume checked field reads and set ok=0 on missing or type mismatch.
value_mapping.vector_reads: Dimensioned values and semantic aliases with compatible underlying types are read by shape and element type. Numeric conversion to the requested C output type is permitted at the C boundary.
value_mapping.quaternion_reads: Stored USD quaternion payloads use component order i,j,k,w. nanousd C API quaternion readers return w,i,j,k and must reorder components explicitly.
value_mapping.matrix_reads: Matrix3d and Matrix4d readers flatten row-major matrix payloads to double[9] or double[16]. Nested row adapter shape is a serialization detail, not a storage requirement.
value_mapping.array_reads: Homogeneous typed arrays are read according to their element type and element shape. Generic heterogeneous arrays and dictionaries are not valid numeric array reads.
value_mapping.string_reads: Returned C strings are owned by the backend handle or stage and remain valid until the owning handle is freed or the stage is closed.
value_mapping.half_reads: Half and half-dimensioned values may be read through float-returning C API functions by numeric conversion at the C boundary; the underlying stored type identity remains half.

laws: the generated backend must export nanousd_create_backend_v1 with C linkage and return a non-null NanousdBackend_v1 pointer

every NanousdBackend_v1 function pointer slot must be non-null; read-only and utility functions point to generated implementations, while mutating or deferred functions point to deterministic unsupported stubs

every generated C ABI function must be total for null handles, invalid handles, null input pointers, out-of-range indices, malformed text, missing values, type mismatches, and unsupported features; failures must be reported through nanousd return conventions rather than process termination
no exception may cross the nanousd C ABI boundary; generated entry points must catch implementation exceptions and return deterministic failure values

functions returning const char* must never return null; missing, invalid, or unsupported string results return a stable empty string unless the nanousd C API explicitly specifies an owning null pointer failure

functions returning caller-owned C strings must return either null on failure or memory compatible with nanousd_free_string; generated code must not return string literals or handle-local buffers from owning string APIs
functions returning opaque handles must return either null or a valid generated handle whose companion functions are total; generated code must not return partially initialized handles
nanousd_open with a null or empty filepath returns null; failed non-null opens may return either null or an invalid stage handle, but every stage function must safely accept both cases
utility functions classified as path, listOp, or math support must not use generic fallback stubs; they must be implemented as typed utility functions with explicit null and invalid-input behavior

the generated C++ target must not depend exclusively on Windows-specific build scripts; it must emit a CMake project that can build on Windows and Linux when a supported compiler and nanousd headers are available

the benchmark-stage-load slice of the generated backend must open supported local USDA files through usd-composed-stage-open and serve nanousd_nprims, nanousd_prim, nanousd_nattribs, nanousd_freeprim, diagnostics, and root-layer/layer-count queries from the resulting populated Stage; the backend must not construct layer-stack, reference, payload, or composition-arbitrator sources directly
read-only C API functions must not mutate the generated stage, layer, namespace source, path table, token table, or field storage except for handle-local caches and C string lifetime buffers

C strings returned by the backend are ABI boundary strings; generated backend storage and query state must retain paths, tokens, field keys, and semantic names through the graph's semantic runtime types

missing values and type mismatches follow nanousd C API conventions: scalar reads set ok=0 and return a neutral value, vector/quaternion/matrix reads return 0, array copy reads return -1, and string reads return null or an empty failure string as specified by the C API
typed attribute reads use document-model checked reads and foundational payload metadata; they must not recover type identity by reparsing canonical JSON dumps
semantic aliases such as point3f, normal3f, color3f, frame4d, and texCoord2f may satisfy C API readers for their underlying typed payload when the requested shape and element type agree
quaternion C API readers must convert USD payload order i,j,k,w to nanousd C API order w,i,j,k
matrix C API readers return row-major flattened arrays
zero-copy arraydata functions may return null until a later contract requires contiguous native storage for the requested array element type
write and create functions in a read-only backend are deterministic unsupported stubs and must not partially mutate stage state
